$comment: Hand-authored taxonomy. Powers the Topic Front Door (/t, /t/:topic) and the section list inside each state hub (/s/:state). See ADR-0022 and TODO/IA-RESET-PLACE-FIRST-WITH-TOPIC-FRONT-DOOR.md. DOCTRINE (user-mandated 2026-05-11): elections are ONE indicator family among many. Social-welfare topics (fiscal, education, health, livelihood) are first-class; elections are not the spine and not the default theme. Order in this array IS the rendering order on /t and inside the state hub.

topics.title: Fiscal capacity
topics.summary: How states raise, borrow, and spend. Debt, deficit, transfers from the Centre. The baseline that contextualises every other state-level number.
topics.notes: Debt and deficit are co-determined by central transfers — the transfers indicators (RBI Net via `fiscal/net_transfers_from_centre`, Rajya Sabha Gross via `fiscal/centre_transfers_gross`) are the contextualisation that makes ranked debt/deficit tables honest. Per ADR-0022 ingest gate. RBI Net covers FY24 Accounts + FY25 RE + FY26 BE; Rajya Sabha Gross covers FY17–FY23 (longer historical run, devolution + grants pre-adjustments). The all-India aggregate equivalent (RBI Net Centre→States transfers, FY08–FY26, 19y) is `fiscal/national_centre_transfers_total` — a country-entity series for the macro envelope view; its data shape is incompatible with the per-state choropleth (it carries one row per FY for `entity_id="IN"`). The four `fiscal/national_*_deficit` indicators (gross fiscal, revenue, primary, primary revenue) come from RBI Appendix Table 1 (Major Deficit Indicators) — same publication as the transfers, all-states aggregate, FY08–FY26 (20y including RE/BE). They answer the macro question 'how much are the states collectively borrowing each year, and what kind of borrowing is it?'.

topics.title: Energy
topics.summary: Where electricity is generated. Installed capacity by state, by fuel.
topics.notes: Installed-MW is a *siting* statistic, not a consumption statistic — the state where a plant sits is not necessarily the state that consumes the power. The indicator artifact's `attribution_geography` field carries this caveat to the renderer. The `installed_capacity_*` family is from CEA's monthly Executive Summary (March 2026 snapshot) and covers all 35 CEA-reported state/UT entities across 7 fuel categories (total, thermal, coal, gas, nuclear, hydro, renewable). The legacy `installed_mw_by_state` indicator (Wikipedia-derived, 4 states only) is retained for back-compat but no longer the default. The state-level family (`state_installed_capacity_*`, `state_electricity_*`, `state_rooftop_solar_capacity_mw`, `state_atc_losses_pct`, `state_acs_arr_gap_inr_per_kwh`) is from the NITI Aayog ICED state-wise deep-dive API and covers 9-11 fiscal years (FY16-FY26) per indicator across all 36 states/UTs plus the All-India aggregate. ICED publishes both 'geographical-location' (where the plant sits) and 'with-allocated-shares' (who has rights to the output) capacity series; we ship both so the analyst can pick the one that matches the question being asked.

topics.title: Elections
topics.summary: Who governs. State Assembly and Lok Sabha results, candidate-level detail, party totals, margins. One indicator family among many — not the spine of the site.
topics.notes: Per ADR-0022 doctrine: elections are NOT the default home-page theme and NOT a featured topic. Social-welfare topics (fiscal, future: education, health, livelihood) are first-class; elections are one of many. Election artifacts have a different schema family (result.summary.schema.json) than indicators — the catalogue's polymorphic `kind` lets them coexist without being demoted in capability or promoted in IA prominence.

topics.title: Economy
topics.summary: How big is each state's economy, in real and nominal terms. GDP and sectoral GVA at constant and current prices, in Lakh Crore Rupees.
topics.notes: Source: NITI Aayog ICED state-wise deep-dive API (which republishes MoSPI National Statistical Office figures with the 2011-12 base year). Covers 10 fiscal years (FY16-FY25) for all 36 states/UTs plus All-India. Constant-price series strips inflation; current-price series is the nominal 'sticker' value. Compare GDP vs Sectoral GVA: GDP includes net product taxes; GVA is the cleaner production-side measure. The 2025-26 row is typically N.A. while NSO finalises that year's accounts. Topic is featured=false pending IA review (the doctrine says social-welfare topics drive IA; pure macro economy is context, not headline).

topics.title: Demography
topics.summary: How many people live where. State population (inter-censal estimates) until the next decadal Census resets the baseline.
topics.notes: Source: NITI Aayog ICED state-wise deep-dive API; underlying figures from MoSPI inter-censal estimates. The most recent Census of India was 2011; the 2021 round was deferred. Per-year deltas in this indicator are projections, not measured population change. Topic is featured=false pending the next Census release; until then this is the denominator for per-capita normalisations on other indicators rather than a headline series in its own right.
